bead.title: docs(operator): document harness-mixing for parallel ddx work shells
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified a gap in the
  operator playbook: when running multiple ddx work --once shells in
  parallel (after ddx-1f774edf lands), operators must understand that
  using the same harness across all N workers will throttle on the
  per-account rate limit (e.g., Anthropic 5-tier rate limits for the
  claude harness). Mixing harnesses across workers (claude + codex +
  openrouter, etc.) dodges per-harness ceilings and delivers true N×
  throughput. There is no operator documentation today that says this.

CURRENT STATE
  - CLAUDE.md describes the project structure and per-CLI commands but
    not the operator playbook for parallel drains.
  - docs/helix/06-iterate/ contains reliability principles and alignment
    reviews but no operator-facing parallel-drain guide.
  - .agents/skills/ddx/reference/ and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/
    contain skill documentation but not an explicit
    parallel-drain-playbook section.
  - CLAUDE.md memory note feedback_models.md says "prefer claude harness
    for execute-loop; avoid agent harness for real work" — the harness
    selection guidance exists for single-worker drain but not for
    parallel.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a short section to CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/ if
  preferred for separation of concerns) titled "Parallel drain
  operator playbook" covering:

  1. When to use parallel drain: after ddx-1f774edf lands; not before
     (lock contention defeats the parallelism today).

  2. How to start: open N tmux panes / shells, each running a
     `ddx work --once` loop. No code change required.

  3. Harness mixing: example of running claude on one shell, codex on
     another, openrouter on a third — mixing dodges per-harness rate
     limits and avoids serializing on Anthropic quota.

  4. Cost ceiling: each ddx work invocation accepts --max-cost; budget
     across N shells.

  5. Conflict expectations: parallel landing produces some preservation
     under load (covered by the chaos sister bead); set operator
     expectation that occasional preservation is normal and the
     preserved iteration refs can be merged manually.

  6. Observability: point at the lock-metrics sister bead's output
     channels (once landed) for debugging contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Building a `ddx work --parallel N` flag (deliberately YAGNI per
    plan review; external shells deliver the same throughput).
  - Server-spawned-worker design (ADR-022 step 7, separate work).
  - Harness-selection algorithm (single-worker memory note already
    covers default selection).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister beads: ddx-1f774edf (must land before parallel drain is
    safe), the chaos test bead, the lock-metrics bead.
bead.acceptance:
1. CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/parallel-drain-playbook.md) contains a "Parallel drain operator playbook" section covering the 6 items in PROPOSED FIX.
2. The doc explicitly notes that parallel drain is unsafe before ddx-1f774edf lands (because of withMainGitLock spanning network I/O).
3. The doc gives a concrete example of mixing harnesses (claude + codex + openrouter or equivalent) across N shells.
4. The doc cross-references the chaos test bead and the lock-metrics bead.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes (markdown lint + secrets + ddx-config validation are the relevant gates for a doc-only change).
